creator: brandon.wandersee@gmail.com
text:
Created attachment 157488
/etc/make.conf file

x11/rofi segfaults on the first keypress when the "run" mode is used. This happens whether started as a daemon and assigned to a keybinding, or called directly from a shell via "rofi -show run." No other mode seems affected. This occurs on a recent install of 10.1-STABLE (roughly one week old).

I've attached my make.conf for clarity's sake, but no unusual messages are logged after segfault (merely a mention of the core dump). There is no mention of the port in /usr/ports/UPDATING.

OS VERSION: 10.1-STABLE r283769
PORT VERSION: 0.15.5
ERROR MESSAGE EXAMPLE: "kernel: [pid] (rofi), uid 1001: exited on signal 11 (core dumped)"
REPRODUCE: Install x11/rofi via ports tree or pkg, execute 'rofi -show run,' and begin typing.
